summary:
Transduction by bacteriophages transfers DNA from a donor *Corynebacterium diphtheriae* cell to recipient cells, altering their characteristics through acquired genes. The process involves bacteriophage infection and subsequent integration of foreign DNA into new host genomes for phenotypic modification. \boxed{Transduction}

The summary focuses only on the critical aspects related to how phages cause genetic modifications specifically within Corynebacterium diphtheriae without extraneous details. It maintains clarity while addressing both components required—mechanism type and its application directly towards understanding gene transfer mechanisms pertinent here.
